bottlenecks.description: 24.1M write operations (87.6% of all events) with mean duration ~15-17 microseconds, indicating extremely small write sizes
bottlenecks.evidence: POSIX write dominates with 24,119,080 operations. Mean write duration across chunks: 13.6-17.2 us. Total POSIX I/O time: 846s.

bottlenecks.description: Checkpoint writes consume significant I/O time despite small individual operations
bottlenecks.evidence: Top 4 files by fhash each have 5.3-6.4M writes with 0 reads, totaling ~24M writes. These are checkpoint files written sequentially.

bottlenecks.description: High metadata overhead from open/close operations during checkpointing
bottlenecks.evidence: 165,540 open operations, 145,008 close operations. Open mean duration ~380-460 us. mkdir: 23,080 ops.

bottlenecks.description: 18 checkpoints written, sizes growing from 56M to 460M, total ~6.5GB. Frequent checkpointing with small interval (0.03 time units)
bottlenecks.evidence: checkpointFileIntervalTime=0.03, tmax=0.5, resulting in 18 checkpoints. Each checkpoint triggers millions of small writes.
